title: A Blended Family's First Christmas Together

all.en: This was our first Christmas as a blended family, and nobody quite knew what to expect.
all.zh: 這是我們組成繼親家庭以後的第一個聖誕節，誰也不太清楚會發生什麼事。

all.en: My mother had remarried a kind man named Steven in late October after dating for two years.
all.zh: 我媽媽在交往兩年後，於十月底和一位名叫史蒂芬的好心男士再婚了。

all.en: Steven brought with him two teenage children, a quiet daughter and a sarcastic younger son.
all.zh: 史蒂芬帶來了兩個正值青春期的孩子，一個沉默的女兒和一個愛挖苦人的小兒子。

all.en: I was twenty-three and had moved out years ago, but I came home for the long holiday week.
all.zh: 我二十三歲，已經搬出去好幾年了，但我趁著長假回家過節。

all.en: The first awkward moment came when we discovered our families had very different Christmas traditions.
all.zh: 第一個尷尬的時刻出現在我們發現兩家有著截然不同的聖誕節傳統時。

all.en: My family opened presents on Christmas morning, but Steven's family always opened them on Christmas Eve.
all.zh: 我們家是聖誕節早上拆禮物，但史蒂芬家總是在聖誕夜就拆。

all.en: Steven's daughter, Hannah, suggested a compromise that nobody had thought of during the heated discussion.
all.zh: 史蒂芬的女兒漢娜在那場激烈的討論中提出了一個沒人想到的折衷辦法。

all.en: Why not open one small gift on Christmas Eve and save the bigger ones for morning?
all.zh: 為什麼不在聖誕夜拆一個小禮物，把大禮物留到早上呢？

all.en: It was such a simple idea that all four of us laughed at how stubborn we had been.
all.zh: 這個點子如此簡單，搞得我們四個都笑自己怎麼會這麼固執。

all.en: The next problem was the Christmas dinner menu, which created its own kitchen drama on Tuesday.
all.zh: 下一個問題是聖誕大餐的菜單，星期二在廚房裡上演了一場小風暴。

all.en: My mother insisted on roast duck because that was what her own mother had cooked every year.
all.zh: 我媽媽堅持要做烤鴨，因為她的母親每年都做這道菜。

all.en: Steven, however, came from a family that always served a turkey with cranberry sauce and stuffing.
all.zh: 然而史蒂芬來自一個一向吃火雞配蔓越莓醬和填料的家庭。

all.en: His sarcastic son, Tyler, said he would eat neither and would survive on snacks instead.
all.zh: 他那個愛挖苦人的兒子泰勒說兩樣他都不吃，他要靠零食過活。

all.en: I volunteered to cook both, even though I had never roasted a turkey in my entire life.
all.zh: 我自告奮勇兩道都做，雖然我這輩子從來沒烤過火雞。

all.en: Hannah said she would help me, and together we watched three different YouTube tutorials that night.
all.zh: 漢娜說她會幫我，那晚我們倆一起看了三支不同的 YouTube 教學影片。

all.en: Cooking side by side in the kitchen turned out to be a wonderful way to get to know her.
all.zh: 在廚房裡並肩做菜，意外地成了認識她最棒的方式。

all.en: She told me about her late mother, who had loved baking cookies shaped like tiny snowflakes.
all.zh: 她跟我說起她過世的媽媽，那位媽媽從前最愛烤雪花形狀的小餅乾。

all.en: I shared stories about my late father, who used to sing carols loudly and slightly off-key.
all.zh: 我則分享了關於我過世父親的故事，他從前總是大聲又有點走調地唱聖誕歌。

all.en: Somewhere between rolling cookie dough and basting the turkey, we became more than just stepsisters.
all.zh: 在揉餅乾麵團和為火雞抹油之間，我們不再只是繼姊妹的關係。

all.en: Tyler was a tougher case because he spent most of the afternoon in his room playing video games.
all.zh: 泰勒比較難搞，因為他一整個下午幾乎都待在房間裡打電動。

all.en: I tried to start a conversation, but every question I asked received a one-word answer.
all.zh: 我試著找話題，但我問的每個問題他都只用一個字回答。

all.en: Finally, I sat on the floor beside him and asked if he could teach me his favorite game.
all.zh: 最後我乾脆坐到他旁邊的地板上，請他教我玩他最愛的遊戲。

all.en: His eyes lit up, and for the next hour I was thoroughly defeated in a racing game.
all.zh: 他眼睛一亮，接下來的一個小時我在一款賽車遊戲裡被徹底打敗。

all.en: By the end, he was laughing and explaining strategies he had spent months perfecting on his own.
all.zh: 到最後，他一邊大笑一邊解釋他自己花了好幾個月才練熟的攻略。

all.en: On Christmas Eve, the five of us gathered around the small tree to open one gift each.
all.zh: 聖誕夜那天，我們五個人圍著那棵小樹，每個人各拆一個禮物。

all.en: Mom opened a handmade photo album from Steven, full of pictures from their two years together.
all.zh: 媽媽拆開的是史蒂芬手作的相簿，裡面滿是他們在一起兩年的照片。

all.en: She cried, and Hannah quietly handed her a tissue without making it into a big moment.
all.zh: 她哭了，漢娜悄悄遞了一張面紙給她，沒有把它變成什麼大場面。

all.en: Tyler opened a vintage video game cartridge from me and actually hugged me for the first time.
all.zh: 泰勒拆開的是我送他的一張復古遊戲卡帶，他還第一次抱了我一下。

all.en: On Christmas morning, the smell of roast duck and turkey mixed in the kitchen like a small miracle.
all.zh: 聖誕節早上，烤鴨和火雞的香味在廚房裡交織，宛如一個小小的奇蹟。

all.en: We squeezed around our dining table that was definitely not designed for five tall adults.
all.zh: 我們擠在那張顯然不是為五個高大成年人設計的餐桌邊。

all.en: Steven asked if we could go around the table and say one thing we were grateful for.
all.zh: 史蒂芬提議我們輪流分享一件自己感謝的事。

all.en: When my turn came, I said I was grateful for unexpected gifts that did not come in boxes.
all.zh: 輪到我時，我說我感謝那些不裝在盒子裡的意外禮物。

all.en: Hannah reached under the table and squeezed my hand without saying a single word in reply.
all.zh: 漢娜在桌子底下握了一下我的手，一句話也沒有回我。

all.en: Tyler joked that he was grateful for someone finally being bad enough at racing to make him look good.
all.zh: 泰勒開玩笑說他很感謝終於有個人賽車爛到能襯托他很厲害。

all.en: As I drove back to the city that evening, I realized our family had quietly grown a little larger.
all.zh: 那天傍晚開車回城裡的路上，我發現我們的家庭已經悄悄變得大了一些。
